markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 80-99 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tightly clustered probabilities across Zelenskyy’s projected X post count for May 5-12, with <20 and 40-59 each at 27% amid recent weeks' output of 64 posts (April 17-24) and 60-79 (April 24-May 1), per resolved markets. This even spread stems from steady communication patterns driven by nightly Russian drone strikes on civilian and energy infrastructure, long-range sanctions updates, and May 1 army reform announcement emphasizing pay hikes and infantry contracts. Absent major catalysts like frontline escalations, diplomatic summits, or NATO summits, volumes hover mid-range; intensified conflict or international endorsements could spike toward 100+, while de-escalation signals might drop below 40.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 29, 2026, FOMC meeting, where the federal funds rate held steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid an 8-4 vote split with hawkish dissents, reinforces trader consensus pricing an 80.5% implied probability of no rate hike through 2026 on Polymarket. The March 2026 dot plot projected median rates remaining in the low-3% range or slightly lower by year-end, reflecting solid economic expansion, ongoing job gains (March nonfarm payrolls +178,000), and inflation trending toward the 2% target without reacceleration signals. Upcoming catalysts include tomorrow's April nonfarm payrolls and May 12 CPI release, alongside the June 17-18 FOMC, though CME FedWatch Tool implies negligible hike risk, aligning with skin-in-the-game sentiment favoring steady or easing policy amid balanced risks.

markets.question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile US-Iran peace negotiations following 2025 airstrikes on Natanz, Fordow, and Isfahan nuclear sites, trader consensus reflects skepticism on near-term US physical custody of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile—estimated at nearly 10,000 kg total by IAEA's February 2026 report, including near-weapons-grade material. President Trump's April claims of Iranian agreement to joint removal were swiftly rejected by Tehran, deeming the uranium "sacred" and non-transferable. Iran began reviewing a US war-ending proposal on May 6, but uranium handover remains a key unresolved red line; Trump demanded a response within one week. Upcoming diplomatic replies or Omani-mediated talks could catalyze movement before the May 31 window closes.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 7 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for this LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch against Ninjas in Pyjamas, fueled by their second-place standing at 7-3 and a five-series win streak, including a dominant 2-0 sweep of NIP on April 22 where jungler Tarzan secured MVP honors with superior map control. NIP languishes at 4-5 in fifth, mired in a three-match skid versus top contenders like BLG and JDG, exposing vulnerabilities in their import-led top-jungle duo of HOYA and Guwon against AL's battle-tested Flandre-Tarzan-Shanks core. No roster changes or injuries reported ahead of this pivotal clash, where AL eyes direct playoff qualification while NIP fights play-in peril.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: FORZE Esports vs Cyber Hero - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between FORZE Esports and Cyber Hero in the BetBoom Rise of Legends 1st Division, initially scheduled for May 7 at 3:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "FORZE Esports" if FORZE Esports win Game 1 against Cyber Hero.

This market will resolve to "Cyber Hero" if Cyber Hero win Game 1 against FORZE Esports.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: CyberHero leads trader consensus as favorites over FORZE Esports in their BetBoom Rise of Legends Season 10 1st Division BO3 matchup on May 7, holding third place at 3-1 (6-3 games, +3 differential) versus FORZE's mid-table 2-2 (5-6, -1). Recent April results highlight CyberHero's edge, with 2-1 and 2-0 wins over bottom teams Aura Farmers and Magic after a lone 0-2 loss to undefeated Team Spirit, matching FORZE's triumphs against the same foes but exposing the latter's vulnerabilities in 1-2 and 0-2 defeats to Virtus.pro and Verso Time. Stable rosters prevail—CyberHero integrating jungler Bert since April 17—amid a double round-robin format; May 5-6 results against Team Yandex, Verso Time, Virtus.pro, and Team Spirit could sway momentum before playoffs.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and Anyone's Legend each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Orbit Anonymo and Lodis each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anonymo Esports, formerly Orbit Anonymo, holds a slight edge at 3-2 in the Rift Legends 2026 Spring group stage standings over LODIS at 2-3, with both teams vying for a top-four playoffs spot in the single round-robin BO3 format. LODIS stunned Anonymo 2-0 just two weeks ago on April 21, showcasing strong macro play from mid laner lee sang and jungler Kokos, but lost 1-2 to undefeated leader Forsaken on May 5. Anonymo rebounded with a 2-0 shutout over DOCISK yesterday, fueled by top laner HeSSZero's MVP performance. LODIS debuts new support Syrpy today, potentially disrupting bot lane synergy, while Anonymo's stable roster of HeSSZero, Joki37, Sobek, ejsner, and Raxxo benefits from recent momentum in this evenly matched rematch.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli leads trader consensus at 39.3% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship after clinching his third consecutive Grand Prix victory from pole at Miami four days ago, extending his points lead to 100-80 over teammate George Russell, whose consistent podiums keep him at 28.5%. Antonelli's streak—starting with his maiden win in China—has capitalized on Mercedes' superior race pace and tire strategy, while Red Bull's balance issues and poor starts have hampered Max Verstappen (6.3%), dropping him to seventh with 26 points. Lando Norris (10.9%) and Charles Leclerc (5.0%) trail amid McLaren and Ferrari's inconsistent qualifying, with the upcoming Canadian Grand Prix looming as a test of momentum.

markets.question: Will Claudia López win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Candidate M a narrow favorite at 49.5% to win Colombia's presidency, with Iván Cepeda Castro at 41.5% and Abelardo de la Espriella at 37.5%, reflecting a closely contested race ahead of the May 31 first-round vote requiring over 50% for outright victory or top-two advancement to runoff. Recent polls, including Yanhaas tracking on May 6 (Cepeda 35%, Paloma Valencia 29%, de la Espriella 19%) and Centro Nacional de Consultoría (Cepeda 37%, de la Espriella 20%, Valencia 16%), confirm no candidate near majority amid high undecideds and right-wing vote split, compounded by March's fragmented congressional elections yielding no party supermajority. Security concerns dominate campaigns, with hardline stances differentiating right contenders; voter consolidation behind one anti-left option or strong debate showings could tip balances toward separation.

markets.question: Will Matt McCarty win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

This elevates mid-tier ball-strikers with strong course history and recent form, including Taylor Pendrith, Patrick Rodgers, David Lipsky (T5 early at -4 thru 13), and Nick Taylor (-5 finished in updates), all bunched at 36-41%. Round 1 rain delay shifted tee times, fueling early volatility with Matt McCarty and Sungjae Im tied at -6, underscoring the competitive parity and upset potential on the long, demanding layout.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $540 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Meta Platforms (META) share price trades around $614 as of May 7, reflecting trader caution following the April 29 Q1 2026 earnings beat—revenue of $56.3 billion (up 33% year-over-year, topping $55.5 billion estimates) and adjusted EPS of $7.31—offset by a raised full-year capital expenditure forecast to $125-145 billion for AI infrastructure, raising margin pressure concerns amid uncertain returns. Ad impressions and pricing drove family of apps revenue growth, but regulatory scrutiny over youth safety adds headwinds. Analyst consensus targets average $830-$840 over the next 12 months, implying 35% upside potential. Key upcoming catalyst: Q2 earnings on July 29, with focus on AI monetization and expense discipline amid sector volatility.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy enters this United21 Playoffs BO3 matchup with momentum from their 2-1 upset win over INFINITE in Season 47's upper bracket quarterfinals on April 9, where they rallied after dropping the opener, showcasing strong map-two and three execution on standard CS2 pool maps. INFINITE, ranked #59 globally with a 71% win rate over the past three months, boasts a nine-match streak from dominating Esplay Elite Gaming Cup and Parken Challenger Championship, bolstered by rifler sl3nd's 1.27 rating and recent roster tweaks including kreaz and Dytor. Imperial Academy (#193, 50% win rate) relies on young core ta9z and aZe (both 1.12 ratings), fresh off European Pro League Cup 4 title with wins over BIG Academy and AaB. Traders weigh INFINITE's form against Imperial's head-to-head edge and playoff pressure in double-elimination format.

markets.question: Pittsburgh Pirates vs. Arizona Diamondbacks: O/U 7.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Pittsburgh Pirates and Arizona Diamondbacks, scheduled for May 7 at 3:40 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Pittsburgh Pirates and Arizona Diamondbacks combine to score 8 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 8, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pittsburgh's Mitch Keller (3-1, 2.85 ERA, 1.05 WHIP) opposes Arizona's Zac Gallen (1-2, 4.45 ERA) in the series finale at Chase Field, pitting the Pirates' superior rotation depth against the Diamondbacks' home advantage amid middling records—Pittsburgh at 20-17 (.541), Arizona 17-18 (.486). Keller's command fuels trader focus on Pittsburgh's edge, tempered by Chris Devenski's fresh 15-day injured list placement for illness, straining the bullpen after a 4-6 stretch in their last 10. Arizona lacks Carlos Santana (groin, 10-day IL), Pavin Smith (elbow, 60-day IL), and others, weakening the lineup, while Jared Triolo returns for Pittsburgh post-injury in this pivotal NL matchup.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 58-59°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Chicago's O'Hare and Midway airports project a daily high temperature of 58-59°F on May 7, 2026, driving the market's overwhelming 99.6% implied probability on this bin amid trader consensus on persistent cool conditions. A recent shortwave trough ushered in a cool Canadian air mass with northwest winds of 10-15 mph, capping boundary layer warming, while scattered showers and mid-level clouds have limited solar insolation despite partial clearing. Model ensembles from NOAA's HRRR and GFS align on this peak near 59°F, well below the May 7 normal of 67°F. Realistic challenges include prolonged sunshine after 2 PM eroding clouds faster than expected, potentially nudging the high to 60°F, though current observations near 50°F midday suggest limited upside as the resolution window closes.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Wuxi hard-court quarterfinals, trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Mark Lajal at 50.5% implied probability against third seed Dane Sweeny, underscoring the matchup's competitive balance between closely ranked right-handers (No. 158 vs. No. 135) with no head-to-head history. Both advanced via grueling three-set round-of-16 wins—Lajal grinding out a 3h49m marathon 7-6(8), 6-7(2), 7-6(2) over veteran Mikhail Kukushkin after straight-setting Renta Tokuda, while Sweeny overcame qualifier Andre Ilagan 6-3, 6-7(2), 6-4 following a dominant R32 over wildcard Rigele Te. Endurance tested on outdoor hard, odds could tip on recovery speed, first-serve effectiveness, or late fitness updates amid mild weather.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 50-54 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the @khamenei_ir account's post count for May 5–12 reflects high uncertainty following a sharp decline in activity since mid-April, with no posts in the past 19 days despite ongoing US-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz highlighted by Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's April 30 statement asserting control amid ceasefire talks. This irregular pattern—bursts around events like Easter (multiple posts April 5) versus prolonged silences post-Ali Khamenei's March martyrdom—keeps probabilities tightly clustered near 25–27% across mid-to-high ranges, including a slight edge for 45–49. Escalation in regional diplomacy, sanctions, or commemorative dates could spur archival statements and boost higher bins; sustained internal leadership transitions or health concerns for Mojtaba might entrench low output below 10.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Tariff" during events with Xi Jinping?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with Xi Jinping from May 14 to May 15, 2026 (https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/04/china/china-us-talks-iran-intl-hnk). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and Xi Jinping from May 14 through May 15, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing for bilateral talks on May 14-15, 2026, marking his first in-person summit since taking office and the first since 2019, after delays tied to U.S. military operations in Iran. Recent White House confirmations and Trump's public remarks—calling the trip "very important," praising Xi as a "tremendous guy," and asserting U.S. leads in AI—signal pragmatic diplomacy amid persistent tensions over tariffs, trade deficits, Taiwan sovereignty, and China's Iran ties. Beijing has elevated Taiwan as a summit priority, while analysts note limited expectations for breakthroughs given entrenched U.S.-China rivalry. Trader sentiment hinges on Trump's rhetorical balance between confrontation and deal-making, with outcomes resolving post-event based on verified statements.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Shai Gilgeous-Alexander at 71% implied probability for Western Conference Finals MVP, reflecting Oklahoma City Thunder's 1-0 lead over the Lakers in the semifinals after a 108-90 Game 1 rout powered by team depth despite SGA's uncharacteristically quiet 18 points on 8-of-15 shooting. markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

The top-seeded Thunder hold home-court advantage and series lead 1-0 despite Jalen Williams remaining out with a hamstring strain. OKC's depth and momentum justify the heavy favoritism.

markets.question: Estudiantes de La Plata vs. Racing Club: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game between Estudiantes de La Plata and Racing Club, scheduled for May 10 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Estudiantes de La Plata and Racing Club combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on afa.com.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for May 10 at 4:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Estudiantes de La Plata enters this Liga Profesional Argentina Apertura Round of 16 matchup atop Group A with 31 points from 16 games and a +12 goal difference, bolstered by home advantage at Estadio Ciudad de La Plata and an unbeaten streak in recent head-to-heads against Racing Club, including a 1-1 draw and multiple wins over the past year. Racing Club, sitting 8th in Group B on 21 points, advanced via a gritty 0-0 draw with Huracán but faces major setbacks: forward Ezequiel Cannavo sidelined by a right rectus anterior muscle tear from that game, midfielder Baltasar Rodríguez suspended for a fifth yellow, and defender Gastón Martirena out with an acromioclavicular sprain. These absences compound Racing's mid-table form, tilting trader consensus toward the hosts amid playoff intensity.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors no Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, at 79.5% implied probability, driven by the absence of a public S-1 filing or listing announcement following January's confidential submission and rumored March debut with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan. Secondary market valuations have slumped to around $7 billion—half the $15 billion 2021 peak—amid monetization hurdles from a gamer-centric user base and compressed SaaS multiples, pricing a sub-$15 billion cap at 13.4% if an offering materializes. Higher brackets languish below 1% as traders price in revenue estimates near $800 million against lofty growth expectations. No imminent catalysts like regulatory progress loom before mid-year resolution.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party at 92.5% implied probability for the IL-09 House seat following Daniel Biss's victory in the crowded March 17 Democratic primary, where he defeated 15 challengers including state Sen. Laura Fine and activist Kat Abughazaleh. This open-seat race in a solidly Democratic district—spanning Chicago's North Shore suburbs like Evanston and Wilmette, with a partisan voting index around D+19—reflects historical blowout margins for Democrats, low GOP baseline support, and Biss's local name recognition as Evanston mayor. John Elleson emerged from the uncompetitive Republican primary. While commanding, odds could shift via late scandals, health issues for Biss, legal developments, or a national Republican midterm wave boosting turnout in battleground races. The general election is November 3.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2026 MLS Goalkeeper of the Year remains razor-tight atop Daniel (49.3%), Jonathan Sirois (49.2%), Novak Mićović (49.2%), and Kristijan Kahlina (48.0%), driven by early-season parity across save percentage, clean sheets per 90 minutes, and goals against average amid roughly 12 matchdays played. San Jose Earthquakes' Western Conference-leading 28 points and league-best defensive record (7 GA) have edged Daniel ahead with 5 shutouts and efficient shot-stopping, while Charlotte's Kahlina paces saves leaders (facing high volume on a 7th-place Eastern side with 19 GA), Sirois impresses post-February trade to 7th-place FC Dallas (16 points, 15 GA), and Mićović stabilizes 10th-place LA Galaxy (13 points, 17 GA). No standout injuries or slumps in the past week sustain the bunched odds, with months left for momentum shifts in Supporters' Shield and playoff races.

If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. markets.question: Will Pierre Gasly win the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 24, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates Kimi Antonelli and George Russell top trader consensus at 32% and 28% implied probabilities for the Canadian Grand Prix winner, reflecting their championship-leading form and superior Mercedes pace on the high-degradation Circuit Gilles Villeneuve. Antonelli, atop the 2026 drivers' standings after three straight wins including Miami, has consistently outqualified and outpaced Russell in recent sessions, with data showing him over half a second quicker per lap on fuel-corrected runs. Lando Norris trails closely at 17.5% amid McLaren's competitive straightline speed, while Max Verstappen's 9.5% accounts for Red Bull's ongoing struggles in qualifying and race pace, keeping the top tightly contested ahead of qualifying.

Fresh Mexico national team call-ups announced April 28 spare Tigres any absences while sidelining five Chivas players, including goalkeeper Raúl Rangel and Roberto Alvarado, though coach Gabriel Milito confirmed returns for José Castillo and Ángel Sepúlveda. Tigres monitor lingering injuries to Marcelo Flores and Vladimir Loroña, but their depth positions them competitively in this high-stakes opener.

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Alavés".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Alavés vs. FC Barcelona - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga standings with a dominant record of 28 wins, 1 draw, and 4 losses after 33 matches, extending their advantage to 11 points over Real Madrid amid a strong run of form. Mid-table Alavés, sitting ninth with 36 points, host the title-chasers at Estadio de Mendizorroza on May 13 in Jornada 36, leveraging home advantage where they've earned solid results. Barcelona's attack remains potent despite season-ending injuries to Lamine Yamal (hamstring) and Andreas Christensen (ACL), with Ronald Araujo nearing return from hamstring discomfort and Marc Bernal recovering from an ankle sprain. Alavés boast no major absences but face stylistic challenges against Barça's possession dominance, as seen in Barcelona's comeback 3-1 win in November; end-of-season rotation and fatigue could sway dynamics.

markets.question: Will between 13 and 15 albums be ranked #1 on the Billboard 200 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of albums that are ranked number 1 on any official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026.

Each specific album may only count once, regardless of the number times it reaches the number 1 spot. Different editions of the same album will be considered to be different specific albums if they contain different tracklists.

Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

All officially published Billboard 200 Albums charts will be treated as final. Revisions made after the release of a Billboard 200 album chart will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart, published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/ and through other official Billboard channels; however, a consensus of credible reporting on albums that reached number 1 on the Billboard 200 within this market's timeframe may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the Billboard 200 number-one albums for 2026 hinges on a brisk early-year pace, with 14 unique titles reaching the top by the May 9 chart—including recent frontrunners like Noah Kahan's debut No. 1 for *The Great Divide* (389,000 units, 2026's biggest streaming week for a rock album), Ella Langley's country surprise *Dandelion*, and BTS's three-week hold on *Arirang*. This diversity across pop, hip-hop, country, K-pop, and rock mirrors recent years' 20-25 annual totals (23 in 2025), driving tight market-implied odds around 19–21 (43%) versus 25+ (41.5%). Key differentiators include H2 heavyweights like Drake's *Iceman*, Playboi Carti's *Baby Boi*, and potential Kanye drops, whose release timing and fan-mobilized streaming could push toward 25+ amid crowded competition, though summer lulls or bundling shifts remain wild cards ahead of year-end charts.

markets.question: UFC: Strickland and Chimaev to Touch Gloves?

Only glove touches occurring in the presence of the referee immediately before the fight begins will qualify.

If the fight is canceled, postponed or it cannot be determined if Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev touched gloves by May 23, 2026, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the official broadcast of the UFC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no glove touch at UFC 328's middleweight title main event between champion Khamzat Chimaev and former champ Sean Strickland, implied by the 73.5% "No" probability, driven by their unprecedented personal animosity during fight week. Strickland recently declared they'll "die enemies" post-fight with no cordiality expected, while escalating threats to shoot Chimaev outside the cage and accusations over leaked training footage have intensified bad blood, skipping traditional faceoffs. Chimaev's responses emphasize cage-only violence as "haram," underscoring stylistic grappling dominance versus Strickland's striking volume but no mutual respect. With the May 9 Prudential Center bout imminent and no injury concerns, this hostile buildup mirrors rare UFC rivalries where pre-fight sportsmanship is absent.

Leeds (43 points) and Crystal Palace (43 points from 34 games) appear safest above the fray, with historical data suggesting 40+ points typically secures survival. Traders eye fixture difficulty, recent form surges, and potential fatigue in this nail-biting table scrap.

Wild's defense remains depleted without top-four blueliner Jonas Brodin (lower-body) and shutdown center Joel Eriksson Ek, both yet to resume skating; veteran goaltender Marc-Andre Fleury is day-to-day (upper-body). Avalanche defenseman Josh Manson (upper-body) missed Game 2 but could return, while winger Joel Kiviranta practiced. Momentum favors Colorado's depth, but Wild's power play and Kaprizov-Boldy duo pose upset threats in a closely contested road matchup.

markets.question: Will Vivek Ramaswamy win the Ohio Governor Republican Primary Election by between 50% and 60%?
markets.description:
The Republican Primary for Governor of Ohio is currently scheduled to take place on May 5, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including those from the Ohio Republican Party.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vivek Ramaswamy's commanding lead in trader consensus for a 60-70% margin of victory in the Ohio Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his landslide win on May 5, capturing 82% of the vote to Casey Putsch's 18% for a 64-point edge, sweeping every county amid strong urban and suburban turnout. Pre-election polls like Bowling Green State University's showed him at 76%, bolstered by Donald Trump's endorsement, a $50 million war chest from self-funding, and America First messaging on tax cuts and energy. With results nearly certified, the wisdom of crowds prices this outcome at 98.7%, though slim chances remain for final tallies revealing minor discrepancies from provisional or absentee ballots to nudge it outside this range.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize following the Norwegian Nobel Committee's April 30 disclosure of 287 nominees—208 individuals and 79 organizations—with Donald Trump likely included via high-profile endorsements from leaders in Israel, Cambodia, and Pakistan. Yulia Navalnaya edges as the slim frontrunner at 7.5% implied probability, buoyed by her anti-Putin activism carrying forward Alexei Navalny's dissident legacy amid Russia's Ukraine aggression; Trump trails closely at 6.5% on past Abraham Accords momentum and fresh nominations, while Volodymyr Zelenskyy holds 6.0% for resilient war leadership. This neck-and-neck dynamic underscores competing global narratives—no dominant campaign or precursor signals yet—with odds poised to shift on summer developments before the October 10 announcement.

markets.question: Will "Canada" be said during the next episode of the All-In Podcast?
markets.description:
The All-In Podcast is scheduled to release episodes every Friday. (https://allin.com/episodes)

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the next released episode of the All-In Podcast. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the next episode of the All-In Podcast added to the official YouTube playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLn5MTSAqaf8peDZQ57QkJBzewJU1aUokl. Specials or other videos posted on the YouTube channel but not included on the All-In Podcast playlist will not be considered.

If no such episode of the All-In Podcast is aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the next episode of the All-In Podcast added to the official YouTube playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLn5MTSAqaf8peDZQ57QkJBzewJU1aUokl. Specials or other videos posted on the YouTube channel but not included on the All-In Podcast playlist will not be considered.

If no such episode of the All-In Podcast is aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Paris be 2°C or below on May 7?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Météo-France observations at Paris-Le Bourget Airport—the official resolution station—confirm the lowest temperature on May 7 reached exactly 9°C during early morning hours under partly cloudy skies and light northerly winds, aligning precisely with the latest ECMWF and GFS model runs that projected overnight lows of 9-10°C amid a persistent high-pressure ridge over western Europe. This stable atmospheric setup, with minimal cloud breaks for radiative cooling limited by urban heat effects, drove trader consensus to 100% on 9°C as the pre-dawn minimum passed without colder readings. Realistic challenges would require an unforecasted cold air intrusion or station data revision in the final daily bulletin, though model ensembles and historical May baselines (averaging 9-11°C) indicate high confidence in this outcome.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a refreshed roster since February featuring riflers Kiryasoo, kidofpain, and s1nside, holds a talent edge in this Exort Series #25 Main Stage BO3 Swiss Round 1 matchup against Wave Esports, who qualified via a 3-1 Contenders Stage run. Wave enters with inconsistent March form—3rd at Esplay Backforce Masters after beating BIG Academy and LEO, but early exits elsewhere—following a March overhaul adding Belof, Katalic, and sheezy to miksozzz. Trader consensus likely reflects academy depth and map versatility versus Wave's Mirage-Inferno strengths (67% win rate each), in a C-Tier event where upsets thrive amid roster flux and standard pool volatility.

markets.question: Israel strike on Yemen by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Yemeni soil or any official Yemen embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Israeli local time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Yemen ground territory or any official Yemen embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Yemen soil is hit by an Israeli missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Yemen territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $375 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit Week of May 4 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit Week of May 4 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla (TSLA) shares have climbed approximately 4% this week through May 7, 2026, reaching an intraday high of $415.83 and closing near $407–$411 amid recovering EV sector sentiment and AI-driven optimism following Q1 results on April 22. Profit rose 17% to $477 million with automotive gross margins rebounding to 21.1%, though deliveries missed estimates and $25 billion in 2026 capex signals negative free cash flow ahead, pressuring the lofty 350x forward P/E multiple. Analyst consensus price targets hover around $400–$406, with highs at $600 tied to Optimus robotics and robotaxi progress. Traders eye Friday's April wage data for macro risks to high-valuation growth stocks, alongside technical resistance at $420–$431.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hungary's parliamentary election on April 12 delivered a landslide defeat to Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party, with Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party securing a supermajority in the National Assembly amid record turnout driven by anti-incumbency sentiment over economic woes and governance critiques. Orbán promptly conceded, ending his 16-year rule and positioning him as the next leader out of power before 2027, reflected in trader consensus pricing him at 99%. All other listed leaders face no comparable near-term threats like snap elections, no-confidence votes, or resignations, keeping their odds minimal. Barring an extraordinary recount, legal reversal, or stalled government formation—scenarios precluded by Tisza's decisive margin—Orbán's exit remains virtually assured.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 120-139 Truth Social posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.
