eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "What will be said on ICEMAN?" markets reflects intense speculation ahead of Drake's ninth studio album drop on May 15, driven by his cryptic Toronto scavenger hunts, ice-block teasers, and minimal traditional promo rollout that echoes past surprise releases like Honestly, Nevermind. Ongoing tension with Kendrick Lamar from prior beefs fuels bets on rival mentions, while high-confidence plays center on recurring themes in Drake's catalog like family ("Daddy") and cultural touchstones ("Covid"), informed by leaked snippets and historical track patterns. No official tracklist or lyrics have surfaced, leaving room for upsets via features hinted by Burna Boy or AI elements. Resolution hinges on post-release transcripts from Spotify and peers, with full album availability by June 30 determining outcomes amid streaming debut competition.

markets.question: Spread: Gwangju FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Gwangju FC" if Gwangju FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Gangwon FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gwangju FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 9 at 3:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gangwon's recent dominance over Gwangju FC, including a 3-0 victory in their earlier K League 1 matchup this season and an unbeaten streak across the last six head-to-heads, underpins trader consensus favoring the visitors despite Gwangju's home advantage at Gwangju Football Stadium. Gwangju languish in 12th place after 10 matches with just six points and a league-worst -16 goal difference, mired in a five-game losing skid marked by defensive frailties (23 goals conceded). Gangwon sit comfortably 6th on 13 points, buoyed by solid recent form (wins in three of last five) and tactical adjustments fueling their mid-table push, though Gwangju's desperation for points could spark an upset in this relegation scrap.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

Key downward pressures include Bright MLS's forecast of a 1% drop in regional median sale prices to $616,700 for 2026, fueled by federal workforce adjustments and persistent mortgage rates above 6%, while countervailing spring demand and modest wage growth in government jobs keep upside alive in higher bins. Resolution hinges on April-May inventory trends and pending economic releases like local nonfarm payrolls.

Anyone's Legend sits at 26% following their Split 1 runner-up finish and tied 7-3 mark in Split 2, with Flandre anchoring a stable roster thriving in the current meta. JD Gaming's 22% reflects their climb to 6-3 amid head coach Tabe's tactical adjustments post-Split 1 fifth place, including key wins over BLG in qualifiers. Top Esports at 13.5% benefits from momentum via upsets like sweeping BLG and AL earlier, though inconsistent BO3 results temper expectations ahead of playoffs.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Joe Wilson's dominant fundraising ($294,000 cash-on-hand) and long tenure in the solidly Republican SC-02 district—R+7 Cook PVI, Trump +14 margin in 2024—drive trader consensus to an 83% implied probability for a GOP general election victory on November 3, 2026. Weak Democratic primary contenders, including Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief with under $2,000 cash each, anchor Democrats at 18%. Candidate markets (A, B near 50¢, Other 50.5%) reflect pre-primary uncertainty ahead of June 9 contests, where Wilson faces minimal GOP challenges from Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond. No major developments in the past 30 days have shifted this stable positioning.

markets.question: Will Trump post "Ceasefire" or "Cease-fire" or "Cease fire" on Truth Social this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution. Sigils at the beginnings of words, such as hashtags, "@" symbols, or denotations of currency, will not disqualify a term from counting towards a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Donetsk Oblast by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia militarily captures the entirety of Donetsk Oblast by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Donetsk Oblast will be considered captured when the entirety of the below-specified municipalities is simultaneously shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If those areas are not simultaneously shaded red by the resolution date, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant municipalities in Donetsk Oblast that must be simultaneously captured in their entirety are: Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Pokrovsk, Dobropillia, Oleksandrivka, Cherkaske, and Sviatohirsk.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of a municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within a municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this may still qualify.

Once Russia has captured the entirety of the above-specified municipalities simultaneously, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will not qualify, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. Similarly, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Control must be established through military conquest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces maintain their long-stated objective to seize the entirety of Donetsk Oblast, focusing attritional assaults and small-group infiltrations on Ukrainian defensive lines around Pokrovsk and the Kostiantynivka-Druzhkivka fortress belt. As of May 6, no confirmed advances occurred despite ongoing operations, with Ukrainian counterattacks regaining positions north of Pokrovsk, including western Bilytske. Geolocated footage shows Russian probes within 1 km of southern Kostiantynivka outskirts following the seizure of Novodmytrivka, but broader April gains by Ukraine—marking Russia's first net territorial loss since 2024—highlight defensive resilience amid Russian communications issues. Moscow's unilateral ceasefire from May 8-9 could temporarily halt momentum, while sustained Western aid remains pivotal for Ukrainian fortifications.

events.title: Knicks vs. 76ers

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks hold a commanding 2-0 lead in the Eastern Conference Semifinals after a 108-102 Game 2 win over the Philadelphia 76ers on May 6, propelled by Jalen Brunson's 26 points including clutch fourth-quarter buckets and a balanced Big Three attack combining for 70 points. Joel Embiid's absence due to right ankle sprain and hip soreness gutted Philly's frontcourt, allowing Knicks dominance on the glass and paint scoring. Heading to Game 3 in Philadelphia on May 8, 76ers gain home-court advantage and desperation, leaning on Tyrese Maxey and Paul George, but Knicks' injury report lists OG Anunoby (hamstring strain) and Josh Hart (thumb sprain) as questionable alongside probable Mitchell Robinson (illness). Knicks' playoff defensive intensity and rebounding superiority continue shaping trader consensus amid Philly's must-win urgency.

markets.question: SC Corinthians Paulista vs. São Paulo FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between SC Corinthians Paulista and São Paulo FC, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both SC Corinthians Paulista and São Paulo FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Corinthians Paulista vs. São Paulo FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the upcoming Clássico Majestoso on May 10 at Neo Química Arena, third-placed São Paulo hold a clear edge over relegation-threatened Corinthians, who sit 16th with a 2-5-4 record and league-low eight goals after 11 Brasileirão Série A matches. Corinthians' trader sentiment is pressured by a muscle injury crisis, with recent April 12 bulletins listing absences like Memphis (thigh), Fabrizio Angileri (hamstring), Kaio César (hamstring), and others, exacerbating their poor scoring form. São Paulo boasts strong recent results and balanced head-to-head history (15 wins apiece in last 47 meetings), though key absences including Jonathan Calleri (concussion protocol) and Lucas Moura (broken rib, late April return) loom. Home crowd intensity and derby unpredictability could fuel an upset for the hosts.

markets.question: Will Ethereum dip to $1,800 in May?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ETH/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 31°C or higher on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated at 19:50 HKT on May 7, projects a maximum temperature of 29°C on May 8 amid mainly cloudy conditions with showers and squally thunderstorms from a trough of low pressure edging closer from northern Guangdong, positioning 29°C as the trader consensus at 47% implied probability. Recent observations confirm this pattern, with May 7 peaking at 29°C under similar hot daytime conditions before moderating monsoon influences. Nearby outcomes like 28°C (22%) and 30°C (21%) reflect uncertainty in shower intensity and cloud cover, which could suppress or allow slight peaks above climatological May norms of 28–29°C. Next forecast update at 11:30 HKT on May 8 may refine these odds as upper-air disturbances evolve.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a highest temperature of 30°C (62.5% implied probability) for May 8 in Singapore, reflecting caution amid the National Environment Agency's (NEA) forecast of thundery showers over many areas in the afternoon, which often cap peak temperatures through reduced solar insolation and increased cloud cover. The NEA's latest update projects a maximum of 34°C, aligning with the first fortnight of May outlook of 33–35°C on most days despite above-average rainfall from inter-monsoon conditions with light, variable winds. Recent days have featured frequent afternoon convection, mirroring May's climatological average high of around 31°C, while inherent forecast uncertainty—driven by convective timing—supports the market's tilt toward moderated outcomes, with new hourly observations expected to refine probabilities as the day unfolds.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Cocaine" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 4, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions—including U.S. interceptions of attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, proposal exchanges, and Trump's optimistic deal comments—amid Saudi pressure and a paused Project Freedom operation, heighten odds for critical phrases like "Mob/Mafia" (63%) targeting perceived adversaries. Lower probabilities for "Ruckus" (17%) or "Daddy" (21%) reflect rarer usage, with traders eyeing upcoming policy meetings, executive order signings, press briefings, and potential gaggles for recorded audio opportunities before market close.

eventMetadata.context_description: My Hero Academia FINAL SEASON commands a dominant 73.5% implied probability as the trader consensus frontrunner for Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 Anime of the Year, fueled by its April 2 nomination alongside 14 other categories—including Best Continuing Series and Animation—coupled with the emotional momentum of concluding its blockbuster saga, which topped global demand charts in 2025. DAN DA DAN Season 2 trails at 17% after leading all nominees with 20 nods, riding breakout critical acclaim and stylistic innovation from its supernatural action. Gachiakuta (7%) and Takopi's Original Sin (6.4%) draw buzz from 16 and strong precursor wins like Anime Corner's 2025 honors, respectively, while The Summer Hikaru Died (2.1%) and The Apothecary Diaries Season 2 (1.7%) lag despite solid reception. With fan voting closed April 14, judge decisions remain unpredictable ahead of the May 23 Tokyo ceremony.

markets.question: Spread: FK Shakhtar Donetsk (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Europa Conference League game, scheduled for May 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FK Shakhtar Donetsk" if FK Shakhtar Donetsk win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Crystal Palace FC vs. FK Shakhtar Donetsk - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Europa Conference League game, scheduled for May 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace hosts Shakhtar Donetsk in the UEFA Europa Conference League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Selhurst Park, following the first leg on neutral turf in Krakow on April 30, where Shakhtar's home games are played amid ongoing conflict. Both sides advanced convincingly in quarters—Palace edging Fiorentina 4-2 aggregate despite a 2-1 second-leg loss, Shakhtar dominating AZ Alkmaar 5-2 overall—with recent domestic form bolstering confidence: Shakhtar's 2-1 win at Zorya on April 23 caps three straight victories as Ukrainian Premier League leaders, while mid-table Palace drew West Ham on April 20 after a dramatic 2-1 stoppage-time triumph over Newcastle. Key concerns include Palace's season-ending hamstring injury to striker Eddie Nketiah and doubts over Adam Wharton and Maxence Lacroix, versus Shakhtar midfielder Isaque's recovery from a head knock against AZ; home advantage and Glasner's tactical setup could prove decisive in this evenly poised tie.

events.title: Feyenoord Rotterdam vs. AZ
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Feyenoord Rotterdam and AZ.

eventMetadata.context_description: Feyenoord's strong home record at De Kuip and second-place Eredivisie standing fuel trader consensus at 54% implied probability, despite a mounting injury list headlined by Jakub Moder's season-ending absence since late April, alongside doubts for Anel Ahmedhodzic, Leo Sauer, and In-beom Hwang. AZ Alkmaar, sitting sixth, enters as a 29% underdog after a resilient 3-3 draw in September's head-to-head, bolstered by fewer injury concerns but challenged by Feyenoord's unbeaten streak in the last eight meetings (seven wins, one draw). The 28% draw pricing underscores the closely contested nature, with both sides prioritizing clean sheets amid late-season table jostling.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the AZ-01 Republican primary market prices former NFL kicker Jay Feely as a heavy 71% favorite to secure the nomination on July 21 for the open R+1 district, propelled by his standout performance in a May 5 candidate forum alongside John Trobough—absent frontrunner rival Joseph Chaplik—and backing from President Trump's January endorsement plus the NRCC's MAGA Majority program. Chaplik, a former Arizona House member who resigned in March to campaign full-time, trails at 19% despite claiming a double-digit lead in an April NextGen poll with 54% undecideds, as traders weigh Feely's superior name recognition and national GOP resources against local legislative experience. Recent ads and forums highlight the crowded field's consolidation around these two amid high non-affiliated voter turnout potential.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prominent Methodist pastor Adam Hamilton's late entry into the Kansas Democratic U.S. Senate primary on April 30 has propelled him to a commanding 74.5% trader consensus, reflecting his rapid $1 million fundraising haul in the campaign's first week and widespread name recognition from leading the nation's largest United Methodist church in Leawood. This surge has eroded prior frontrunner state Sen. Patrick Schmidt's position to 11.3%, despite Schmidt's earlier Q1 fundraising lead among Democrats, as traders prioritize Hamilton's momentum in a low-turnout August 4 primary. Minor candidates like Sandy Spidel Neumann (5.6%) and Christy Davis (2.4%) trail amid sparse polling, with markets emphasizing Hamilton's outsider appeal and potential to consolidate support against Republican incumbent Roger Marshall in November.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects skepticism that any AI model will first reach 1550 Elo on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena in 2026, with "None" at 58.5% implied probability, as the current frontier hovers around 1503—led by Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 since its mid-April release that claimed the top text leaderboard spot and dominated code arenas. This ~47-point gap underscores scaling challenges amid compute limits and diminishing returns on larger language models, despite competitive pressure from OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (now mid-pack), Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro, and open challengers like Z.ai's GLM-5.1. Anthropic's consistent leadership and rumored Mythos preview fuel its 25% edge, while upcoming unreleased flagships from xAI and DeepSeek offer low-odds upside before year-end.

markets.question: Will Google have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

The April party affiliation window bolstered PL's roster and aligned it with opposition momentum amid Supreme Federal Court tensions and Flávio Bolsonaro's tight presidential polls against Lula. markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

Carlos Alcaraz sits at 19% after completing his career Grand Slam with the Australian Open triumph but faltering in recent Masters finals to Sinner. Novak Djokovic's 9.9% reflects ongoing shoulder injury woes, with withdrawals from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid limiting match practice at age 38. Jack Draper and Alexander Zverev at 3.5% each draw support from recent upsets—Draper over Djokovic at Indian Wells—and consistent top-5 contention, though grass-court history favors the top trio amid a wide-open field post-French Open.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include National + ACT + NZF + Maori?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Khachanov vs. Shevchenko: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Karen Khachanov and Alexander Shevchenko in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP statistics.

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Karen Khachanov vs Alexander Shevchenko
events.description:

This market will resolve to 'Karen Khachanov' if Karen Khachanov advances against Alexander Shevchenko.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Shevchenko' if Alexander Shevchenko advances against Karen Khachanov.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Shevchenko's gritty 7-6(5) third-set victory over Camilo Ugo Carabelli in the previous round at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia underscores his clay-court resilience this spring, driving trader consensus to a 59% implied probability in his favor despite lower ranking (No. 85). Khachanov, seeded around No. 13 with a 2-1 head-to-head edge—including straight-sets wins at Rome 2024 and Dubai 2026 hard courts—enters as underdog amid dismal 2026 clay form (1-3 record), lacking recent momentum on the slow Foro Italico surface where Shevchenko's baseline consistency and movement shine. No injury concerns reported for either, keeping focus on matchup dynamics and rest advantages.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's (KMA) short-term forecast, updated May 7, projects clear skies and a 22°C high for Seoul on May 9, yet trader consensus favors 20°C (33.5%) and 19°C (29%) amid ensemble model spreads showing 2–3°C variability from global forecasts like GFS and ECMWF. Differentiating factors include potential high cloud intrusion or light northerly winds capping diurnal heating at 19°C, consensus subsidence and solar insolation yielding 20–21°C, or unhindered boundary layer mixing pushing 22°C+. Early May climatology averages 20–21°C highs, with recent actuals (e.g., May 8 forecast at 20°C) reinforcing caution; KMA's next update later today could sharpen odds as observations refine short-range uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

The latest EIA report revealed a 2.3 million barrel draw in US crude inventories for the week ended May 1—smaller than the expected 2.8 million barrel decline—highlighting persistent supply tightness despite weak Chinese demand forecasts projecting minimal growth through mid-year. Trader sentiment balances elevated risk premiums against bearish global inventory builds and potential US Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases. Key catalysts ahead: weekly EIA stock updates through June, China's May import figures, and the OPEC+ ministerial meeting on June 7.

markets.question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit (HIGH) $216 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for NVIDIA (NVDA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the NVIDIA (NVDA) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NVDA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire following the 2026 Iran war, US-Iran indirect negotiations mediated by Pakistan in Islamabad have regained momentum, with US envoy Steve Witkoff confirming ongoing conversations as Tehran reviews Washington's latest proposal for ending hostilities and curbing its nuclear program. President Trump signaled optimism for a one-page memorandum of understanding to enable 30 days of deeper talks but cautioned the Iranian response may prove unacceptable. Sources point to a potential resumption next week, likely featuring mid-level diplomats rather than principals like the Secretary of State or Iranian Foreign Minister, absent a major breakthrough in bilateral tensions. Traders monitor official announcements on participants, as escalation signals or diplomatic progress could elevate attendance levels.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold trader consensus at 50.5% implied probability to win the Eastern Conference after surging to a 2-0 lead over the Philadelphia 76ers in the conference semifinals, capped by a 108-102 Game 2 victory at Madison Square Garden on May 6 that showcased Jalen Brunson's scoring and OG Anunoby's defensive impact amid a raucous home crowd. Detroit Pistons follow at 33.1% following their 1-seed regular-season dominance (60-22 record) and Game 1 triumph over the Cleveland Cavaliers on May 5, leveraging Cade Cunningham's playmaking in a tight matchup. Cavaliers sit at 13.0% despite surviving first-round drama via Game 7, but trail 0-1 with questions on road resilience; 76ers languish at 3.5% facing historical 0-2 series elimination odds near 80% for road teams. No major injury reports alter lineups as Game 2s loom.

markets.question: Will Khamzat Chimaev be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Ilia Topuria at 68.5% implied probability to become UFC pound-for-pound #1 in 2026, driven by his undefeated 17-0 record and recent conquest of the lightweight title via submission over Charles Oliveira at UFC 317, elevating him to #2 in official rankings. Current P4P king Islam Makhachev solidified his 28-1 dominance with a unanimous decision win over Jack Della Maddalena earlier this year, propping "Islam/None in 2026" to 26% as traders doubt a swift dethroning. Khamzat Chimaev's 19.4% reflects his #3 slot and hype ahead of the May 9 middleweight title defense against Sean Strickland at UFC 328, where a statement win could vault him. The May 5 rankings update post-Fight Night showed no top P4P shifts, while contenders like Petr Yan (12.3%) and Arman Tsarukyan (9.3%) lag due to divisional focus amid Topuria's cross-weight trajectory and upcoming Gaethje clash on June 14.

markets.question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $2,000 on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will Enzo Fernández be included in Argentina's official 2026 World Cup squad list?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed player is announced as a member of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

Only full squad announcements will be considered. Prior cut lists or previous squad lists other than the officially announced squad lists will not be considered.

If a player is officially announced as part of the squad but is replaced before the nation’s first game for any reason, the corresponding market will still resolve “Yes”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the listed player cannot be confirmed as a part of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Lee Jung-hyun win the 2026 Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Min Hyung-bae at 97.7% implied probability to win the June 3, 2026, inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayoral election, reflecting the Honam region's longstanding status as a Democratic Party stronghold where opposition People Power Party candidates historically poll below 20-30% amid high progressive turnout. Min, a sitting National Assembly member from Gwangju, unified the Democratic nomination in mid-April after a competitive runoff primary victory over Jeollanam-do Governor Kim Young-rok, consolidating party support without internal fractures. PPP's Lee Jung-hyun, former party chair with regional ties, recently proposed debates on integration and economic issues but faces structural barriers in this deep-blue battleground. Upsets would require a major Min scandal, national political shifts, or anomalous voter mobilization before election day.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a 99% implied probability that 2026 peak CPI inflation will exceed 3.5%, with 77% odds above 4%, reflecting March 2026 CPI surging to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—and core PCE at 3.2%, driven by energy costs up 10.9% and persistent service inflation amid tariff implementations and supply disruptions. This contrasts with March FOMC median projections of 2.7% PCE for 2026, highlighting trader skepticism on disinflation amid robust labor markets and unit labor costs rising 2.3% in Q1. Key catalyst ahead: April CPI release on May 12, alongside Fed speeches today from Kashkari and Williams, which could signal policy shifts as rates hold at 3.50%–3.75%.

markets.question: Will Zendaya and Tom Holland get married by December 31?

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Zendaya and Tom Holland, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket places a 67.9% implied probability on Zendaya and Tom Holland tying the knot by December 31, driven by their confirmed engagement since early 2025 and a string of tantalizing unverified hints, including stylist Law Roach's March 2026 claim at the SAG Actor Awards that "the wedding has already happened." Recent couple sightings, like Zendaya supporting Holland at a May padel match while sporting a gold band and his rare comments on their shared crocheting hobby at home, underscore their stable, private relationship amid busy careers in Challengers follow-ups and Spider-Man projects. Zendaya's April interviews emphasizing privacy and "parasocial boundaries" temper full certainty, leaving room for a low-key ceremony or further delays, with no official announcement as the key swing factor.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone inflation accelerated to 3% in April 2026—highest since September 2023—despite Q1 GDP expanding a meager 0.1% quarter-on-quarter, leading the ECB to hold deposit rates at 2.00% and the main refinancing rate at 2.15% on April 30 while hinting at a possible June hike amid sticky price pressures. This has bolstered EUR/USD to around 1.173, with trader consensus pricing in reduced ECB-Fed rate differentials as US leading economic indicators weakened 0.6% in March. Key swing factors include upcoming Eurozone CPI revisions, US nonfarm payrolls, and FOMC projections on June 16-17, alongside ECB's June 10 policy meeting, potentially targeting technical levels near 1.180 resistance or 1.150 support.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The open Texas 38th Congressional District seat, vacated by incumbent Wesley Hunt's U.S. Senate bid, drives trader consensus heavily favoring the Republican Party at 81.5% implied probability, consistent with the district's Solid Republican Cook rating and R+12 partisan lean. Hunt secured 63% victories in 2022 and 2024 against Democrat Melissa McDonough, now the nominee after her March 3 primary win. Republicans Jon Bonck (Trump-endorsed, leading primary at 48%) and Shelly deZevallos advanced to the May 26 primary runoff, with Bonck's $1.5 million fundraising edge over deZevallos ($930,000) and McDonough ($48,000) underscoring GOP strength. No recent polls show a competitive general election on November 3, though the runoff winner could solidify party momentum in this Houston-area stronghold.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Bennie Thompson's easy renomination in the March 10 Democratic primary against challengers Evan Turnage and Pertis Williams has reinforced trader consensus favoring a Democratic hold on Mississippi's 2nd Congressional District, rated Solid Democratic by nonpartisan forecasters due to its majority-Black electorate and consistent partisan voting patterns. Republican nominee Ron Eller, who eked out a narrow primary win over Kevin Wilson, confronts steep barriers including Thompson's three-decade incumbency, fundraising edge, and the district's historical margins exceeding 20 points. Absent polling shifts or redistricting following recent Voting Rights Act rulings—which traders price as unlikely before the November 3 general election—79% odds capture the entrenched local dynamics and skin-in-the-game assessment of minimal GOP path to victory.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on tech acquisitions before 2027 hinges on a record M&A surge in AI infrastructure and photonics, with Big Tech hyperscalers aggressively consolidating for compute and optics capabilities amid relaxed antitrust scrutiny. Recent catalysts include Marvell's acquisition of Polariton Technologies for plasmonics-based silicon photonics to enable 3.2T AI networking, signaling broader co-packaged optics (CPO) dealmaking, and speculation around laser supplier Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) feeding into hyperscaler supply chains like POET, Ayar Labs, and Jabil. iRobot remains a prime target post-Amazon deal collapse, while AI coding platform Cursor draws bidder interest from players like SpaceX or xAI for developer tools. Upcoming earnings calls and TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 panels could reveal strategic partnerships or bids, though timelines often slip due to regulatory reviews.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli leads trader consensus at 39.3% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship after clinching his third consecutive Grand Prix victory from pole at Miami four days ago, extending his points lead to 100-80 over teammate George Russell, whose consistent podiums keep him at 28.5%. Antonelli's streak—starting with his maiden win in China—has capitalized on Mercedes' superior race pace and tire strategy, while Red Bull's balance issues and poor starts have hampered Max Verstappen (6.3%), dropping him to seventh with 26 points. Lando Norris (10.9%) and Charles Leclerc (5.0%) trail amid McLaren and Ferrari's inconsistent qualifying, with the upcoming Canadian Grand Prix looming as a test of momentum.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include Labour + Green?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Obras vs. San Lorenzo
markets.description:
In the upcoming LNB game, scheduled for February 12 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Obras win, the market will resolve to "Obras".
If the San Lorenzo win, the market will resolve to "San Lorenzo".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Obras Sanitarias' dominant home form at Estadio Luis Conde and superior overall record of 20-14 entering their April 18 matchup against struggling San Lorenzo (13-22) underpin the trader consensus of 78.5% implied probability in La Liga Nacional de Básquet. Obras, vying for a top-six playoff seed, capitalized on recent head-to-head wins, including a December 2025 victory, while San Lorenzo languished near the bottom, having clinched 16th place and missing playoffs. No major injuries reported disrupted key rotations, amplifying Obras' matchup advantages in pace and defensive efficiency against a road-weary foe facing elimination pressure.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: April 2026 CPI rose 2.6% year-over-year—the fastest pace in 21 months—driven by a 7.9% monthly surge in petroleum products amid elevated global oil prices, accelerating from March's 2.2% and lifting trader sentiment toward higher bins on Polymarket. Market-implied probabilities cluster tightly around a mid-2% December 2026 CPI year-over-year reading, with the 2.7%-2.9% outcome at 45.5% narrowly leading 2.4%-2.6% (41.3%) and 2.1%-2.3% (40.2%), reflecting debate on inflation persistence versus Bank of Korea's 2% target and prior 2.1% forecasts. Key differentiators include oil price trajectories, base effects, and core CPI stability near 2.2%; BoK's May 28 policy meeting could shift odds amid deputy governor signals for potential rate hikes from 2.5%.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include Labour?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Will Dawn Buckingham win the 2026 Texas Republican Primary?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A University of Houston poll released May 5 shows Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton leading incumbent Sen. John Cornyn by three points among likely Republican primary runoff voters, bolstering Paxton's trader consensus edge ahead of the May 26 contest. Following the March 3 primary where neither candidate exceeded 50%—Cornyn at around 42%, Paxton near 41%—the runoff pits the Trump-aligned challenger, who attacks Cornyn's establishment record including the 2022 Safer Communities Act, against the seasoned senator boasting over $8 million cash-on-hand and institutional support. Paxton's grassroots momentum among conservative base voters, amplified by recent polls like TPOR's April 8-point Paxton lead, outweighs Cornyn's financial advantage in current pricing, with early voting set for May 18-22 potentially decisive in this closely contested GOP nomination battle.
